verses.2: “No doubt, but you are the people,
verses.3: But I have understanding as well as you;
verses.4: I am like one who is a joke to his neighbor,
verses.5: In the thought of him who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune.
verses.7: “But ask the animals now, and they will teach you;
verses.8: Or speak to the earth, and it will teach you.
verses.9: Who doesn’t know that in all these,
verses.10: in whose hand is the life of every living thing,
verses.14: Behold, he breaks down, and it can’t be built again.
verses.15: Behold, he withholds the waters, and they dry up.
verses.16: With him is strength and wisdom.
verses.18: He loosens the bond of kings.
verses.20: He removes the speech of those who are trusted,
verses.22: He uncovers deep things out of darkness,
verses.23: He increases the nations, and he destroys them.
verses.24: He takes away understanding from the chiefs of the people of the earth,
verses.25: They grope in the dark without light.
